content.full_text:
January 31, 1992

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Re: Conflict of Interest Ruling

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

I spoke with attorney John Wallace of your office on January 29, 1992, for the purpose of obtaining an emergency opinion with regard to a potential conflict of interest situation. I am a member of the Fresno County Planning Commission and am self-employed as an Attorney at Law. The specific question I posed to Mr. Wallace deals with whether or not a conflict of interest results if an attorney refers a client to me and then the attorney appears before the Fresno County Planning Commission.

In this specific instance, a potential client was referred to me by an attorney who does not practice in the area of family law. The referring attorney provided the potential client with the names of two or three family law attorneys and the client then chose to call me and retain me. I have never paid a referral fee to a referring attorney and it is not my practice to do so. The referring attorney specializes in the area of land use planning and often appears before the Fresno County Planning Commission as the attorney for applicants who have matters before the Commission.

My specific question to Mr. Wallace, then, was whether or not I should disqualify myself as a result of a conflict of interest if the referring attorney were to appear before the Planning Commission after I had been retained by a client referred to me by this referring attorney.

Mr. Wallace did some preliminary research and spoke with me by telephone on January 29. My understanding of my conversation with Mr. Wallace led me to believe that, pursuant to FPPC Regulation #18704.3, no conflict of

Scott Hallabrin  
Re: Conflict of Interest Ruling  
January 31, 1992  
Page 2

interest results as long as I pay no referral fee to the referring attorney. Mr. Wallace indicated that the above-cited regulation, while not specifically on point, is analogous to the question I have posed, since the above-cited regulation applies to real estate brokers and other similar professionals.

It is my understanding, therefore, that no conflict of interest is present if I am hired by the client and if the referring attorney then appears before the Fresno County Planning Commission, as long as no referral fee is paid to the referring attorney. I would appreciate it if you would provide me with a written opinion to confirm the oral advice I received from John Wallace on January 29. Please submit your written opinion, at the earliest date possible, to me at the address on this letterhead. Of course, if I have misunderstood any of the information given to me by Mr. Wallace or if you have any other information which will be of help to me, please advise me of such in your opinion letter.

Thank you for your kind assistance in this regard.

Very truly yours,

[Signature]
DEBRA J. KAZANJIAN

DJK/vat
